Name: tensor_core_kv_cache_layouts
Title: TensorCore-Optimized KV-Cache Layouts for Native FP16 Inference
Short Hypothesis: KV-cache layouts can be optimized for tensor-core utilization in native FP16 precision by aligning memory access patterns with tensor-core warp-level parallelism, achieving significant speedups without quantization.
Related Work: BitDecoding (Du et al., 2025) demonstrates the benefits of tensor-core utilization for low-bit KV-cache decoding but relies on quantization. Spectral Quantization Routing (internal) explores dynamic precision allocation but does not address layout optimization. Our work distinguishes itself by focusing on native FP16 layouts, avoiding quantization overhead while maximizing tensor-core efficiency.
Abstract: The growing memory and bandwidth demands of autoregressive decoding in large language models (LLMs) necessitate efficient KV-cache utilization. While existing work focuses on low-bit quantization to reduce memory footprint, we propose a novel approach: optimizing KV-cache layouts for tensor-core utilization in native FP16 precision. By aligning memory access patterns with tensor-core warp-level parallelism, we achieve significant speedups without the overhead of quantization. We introduce a systematic framework for designing tensor-core-friendly layouts, including warp-coalesced memory access, bank-conflict-free addressing, and tensor-core-aligned padding. Our method is evaluated on modern GPU architectures (Ampere, Hopper) using end-to-end latency and throughput metrics. Experiments on LLaMA and GPT models demonstrate up to 3.5× speedup over FP16 FlashDecoding-v2, with negligible accuracy loss. This work bridges the gap between memory-bandwidth optimization and tensor-core utilization, offering a practical, quantization-free path to efficient LLM inference.
Experiments: Baseline Comparison: Measure end-to-end latency and throughput of FlashDecoding-v2 with FP16 KV-cache on Ampere and Hopper GPUs.
Layout Optimization: Implement tensor-core-aligned KV-cache layouts, including warp-coalesced memory access, bank-conflict-free addressing, and padding for tensor-core efficiency.
Profiling: Collect Nsight traces for occupancy, achieved bandwidth, warp stall reasons, and arithmetic intensity before and after optimization.
A/B Testing: Compare latency (p50/p99), throughput under batching, and energy per token for optimized layouts vs. baseline.
Accuracy Validation: Evaluate accuracy on standard benchmarks (e.g., LAMBADA, HellaSwag) to ensure no degradation from layout changes.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Risk: Tensor-core utilization may be limited by memory bandwidth, reducing potential speedups.
Limitation: The approach assumes modern GPU architectures with tensor cores; older GPUs may not benefit.
Risk: Layout changes may introduce overhead for small batch sizes or short sequences.
Limitation: The method is specific to autoregressive decoding; other inference scenarios may require different optimizations.
